---
title: "codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [5, 8, 5, 15, 15, 16, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-5-vs-day-0 codon Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control) and columns are ribosome sites (A-site / P-site / E-site, left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against log2(odds ratio) on the x-axis (x range about -1 to +1.5); each glyph is one codon, coloured by the physicochemical class of its encoded residue (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple, stop = grey). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is log2(OR) = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/composite/codon_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one codon at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of ~62 (61 sense codons plus the in-frame stop TGA); within a panel a glyph to the right of zero is enriched at day_5 relative to day_0 within that condition, to the left is depleted, and higher up the panel means a smaller FDR. The two rows let the same (site, codon) cell be read in BWM and control side by side.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv` (aa-level parent of this contrast)
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), within-condition day_5-vs-day_0 contrast per (condition, site, codon); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of ~62 codons
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, codon) 2x2 day-5-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of ~62 codons (372 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 reps per (condition,timepoint) summed before Fisher. Same anti-conservative concern as per-timepoint Fisher.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are whole-transcriptome; tiny effects yield tiny p-values; odds_ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is per (condition,site) within each contrast file (~20 aa or ~61 codon tests per family).
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition and between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, these tests do NOT mix conditions or timepoints; each Fisher is held within one (condition,contrast) cell. Structurally cleaner on the pooling-as-confound axis; positive design feature.

---

## Headline

At codon-level within-condition Fisher day_5 vs day_0, 329/372 tests clear FDR<0.05 (BWM 154/186, control 175/186) — essentially tied with the day_10_vs_day_0 codon file (330) for the family's highest codon count: 144 site x codon cells significant in both conditions, 10 BWM-only, 31 control-only. The minimum adjusted p underflows to 0.0 (20 cells) at whole-transcriptome pooled N (totals 2.0M-3.4M); the informative axis is `effect change`, not p.

The in-frame stop codon TGA is enriched at day_5 relative to day_0 in both conditions (E:TGA BWM `log2_OR`=+1.195 / control +0.701; P:TGA BWM +1.111 / control +0.909; A:TGA BWM +0.503 / control +0.466), with the P and E cells flagged `low-count` in BWM (unstable — see caveats). Like its amino-acid sister, this contrast is more coordinated than day_10_vs_day_0 / day_10_vs_day_5: large shared-direction cells include A:GGC both depleted (BWM -0.469 / control -0.244), A:CCA both depleted (-0.169 / -0.490), P:GGA both depleted (-0.399 / -0.311), A:GAT both enriched (+0.347 / +0.170), A:AAT both enriched (+0.298 / +0.329), A:GTT both enriched (+0.278 / +0.042). Reported at equal billing, the largest BWM-vs-control divergences are A:AGG (BWM -0.202 / control +0.364), E:AGG (BWM -0.137 / control +0.394), E:TCC (BWM +0.212 / control -0.296), A:ACC (BWM +0.144 / control -0.287).

## Top hits

`log2_OR` is the within-condition Fisher effect for the day_5 vs day_0 contrast: positive = enriched at day_5 relative to day_0 within that condition, negative = depleted. Each row pairs the BWM and control value for one (site, codon) cell; `effect change` = BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`. A value near zero means both conditions moved together from day_0 to day_5 (a shared trajectory); a large-magnitude value means the two conditions' day_5-vs-day_0 trajectories diverge (positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM). The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in A -> P -> E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending (no row cap); cells significant in neither condition are omitted. Because the pooled per-condition totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts, almost every shown cell clears FDR<0.05 and p magnitude is uninformative. The cells that matter most are therefore those with the largest `|effect change|` (the divergences at the top and bottom of each site block), not those with the smallest p. The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon; the `flag` column carries `low-count`, which in this file fires only on the in-frame stop codon TGA.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions (n = 144 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | ACC | T | +0.144 | -0.287 | +0.431 |  |
| A | GTC | V | +0.186 | -0.149 | +0.336 |  |
| A | CCA | P | -0.169 | -0.490 | +0.320 |  |
| A | TCC | S | -0.030 | -0.331 | +0.302 |  |
| A | GCT | A | +0.053 | -0.213 | +0.266 |  |
| A | CGT | R | +0.064 | -0.194 | +0.259 |  |
| A | CTT | L | +0.025 | -0.218 | +0.242 |  |
| A | GTT | V | +0.278 | +0.042 | +0.236 |  |
| A | GAC | D | +0.108 | -0.123 | +0.231 |  |
| A | GAT | D | +0.347 | +0.170 | +0.177 |  |
| A | ATC | I | +0.173 | +0.041 | +0.132 |  |
| A | CAC | H | -0.118 | -0.250 | +0.132 |  |
| A | AAC | N | +0.182 | +0.090 | +0.092 |  |
| A | GAG | E | +0.028 | -0.016 | +0.044 |  |
| A | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +0.503 | +0.466 | +0.036 |  |
| A | ACT | T | +0.096 | +0.071 | +0.025 |  |
| A | GGA | G | -0.228 | -0.243 | +0.015 |  |
| A | ATT | I | +0.228 | +0.242 | -0.014 |  |
| A | CAA | Q | -0.043 | -0.023 | -0.020 |  |
| A | AAT | N | +0.298 | +0.329 | -0.031 |  |
| A | TTT | F | +0.130 | +0.171 | -0.041 |  |
| A | CCT | P | -0.096 | -0.053 | -0.044 |  |
| A | ATG | M | +0.186 | +0.230 | -0.044 |  |
| A | AAG | K | -0.071 | -0.020 | -0.051 |  |
| A | CAT | H | +0.048 | +0.106 | -0.058 |  |
| A | CCC | P | -0.348 | -0.269 | -0.079 |  |
| A | TCA | S | +0.079 | +0.159 | -0.080 |  |
| A | GGG | G | -0.186 | -0.079 | -0.107 |  |
| A | TCG | S | -0.039 | +0.070 | -0.109 |  |
| A | TAC | Y | -0.360 | -0.241 | -0.118 |  |
| A | GGT | G | -0.232 | -0.112 | -0.120 |  |
| A | TAT | Y | +0.096 | +0.250 | -0.155 |  |
| A | AGA | R | -0.050 | +0.107 | -0.157 |  |
| A | GTG | V | -0.292 | -0.133 | -0.159 |  |
| A | TGC | C | -0.315 | -0.125 | -0.190 |  |
| A | TGT | C | +0.134 | +0.328 | -0.194 |  |
| A | TTG | L | -0.251 | -0.043 | -0.209 |  |
| A | CTG | L | -0.148 | +0.061 | -0.209 |  |
| A | AGT | S | +0.124 | +0.335 | -0.212 |  |
| A | CTA | L | -0.153 | +0.059 | -0.212 |  |
| A | GCA | A | -0.290 | -0.077 | -0.213 |  |
| A | GGC | G | -0.469 | -0.244 | -0.225 |  |
| A | CGG | R | -0.120 | +0.154 | -0.274 |  |
| A | CGA | R | -0.056 | +0.220 | -0.276 |  |
| A | TGG | W | -0.538 | -0.255 | -0.284 |  |
| A | ATA | I | +0.135 | +0.427 | -0.292 |  |
| A | AGG | R | -0.202 | +0.364 | -0.566 |  |
| P | ACC | T | +0.027 | -0.338 | +0.365 |  |
| P | CAC | H | +0.037 | -0.302 | +0.339 |  |
| P | AAG | K | -0.064 | -0.368 | +0.305 |  |
| P | TCC | S | +0.114 | -0.188 | +0.302 |  |
| P | CGC | R | +0.057 | -0.227 | +0.284 |  |
| P | CTC | L | +0.162 | -0.119 | +0.281 |  |
| P | CCA | P | -0.144 | -0.401 | +0.257 |  |
| P | GCC | A | -0.075 | -0.305 | +0.230 |  |
| P | TAC | Y | +0.048 | -0.179 | +0.227 |  |
| P | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +1.111 | +0.909 | +0.202 | low-count (BWM) |
| P | ATG | M | +0.154 | -0.041 | +0.195 |  |
| P | AGA | R | -0.037 | -0.198 | +0.161 |  |
| P | CAG | Q | +0.030 | -0.130 | +0.160 |  |
| P | TTG | L | +0.174 | +0.075 | +0.099 |  |
| P | CAT | H | +0.116 | +0.024 | +0.092 |  |
| P | CTT | L | +0.132 | +0.054 | +0.078 |  |
| P | CGT | R | -0.035 | -0.107 | +0.073 |  |
| P | CTG | L | +0.194 | +0.143 | +0.051 |  |
| P | ATC | I | +0.022 | -0.020 | +0.042 |  |
| P | CCG | P | +0.086 | +0.061 | +0.025 |  |
| P | ACT | T | +0.044 | +0.045 | -0.001 |  |
| P | TTA | L | +0.332 | +0.335 | -0.003 |  |
| P | AAA | K | +0.044 | +0.047 | -0.004 |  |
| P | AGC | S | +0.067 | +0.074 | -0.007 |  |
| P | ATA | I | +0.252 | +0.260 | -0.009 |  |
| P | TAT | Y | +0.086 | +0.102 | -0.017 |  |
| P | ACA | T | +0.054 | +0.083 | -0.029 |  |
| P | TCT | S | +0.065 | +0.106 | -0.041 |  |
| P | GAC | D | -0.115 | -0.071 | -0.044 |  |
| P | ACG | T | +0.183 | +0.238 | -0.056 |  |
| P | TCG | S | +0.182 | +0.240 | -0.058 |  |
| P | TCA | S | +0.090 | +0.147 | -0.058 |  |
| P | GAA | E | +0.040 | +0.121 | -0.082 |  |
| P | GGA | G | -0.399 | -0.311 | -0.088 |  |
| P | CGG | R | +0.098 | +0.196 | -0.098 |  |
| P | CCT | P | +0.087 | +0.218 | -0.131 |  |
| P | AAT | N | +0.159 | +0.290 | -0.131 |  |
| P | ATT | I | +0.087 | +0.223 | -0.137 |  |
| P | AGT | S | +0.034 | +0.186 | -0.152 |  |
| P | TGT | C | +0.129 | +0.288 | -0.159 |  |
| P | GTA | V | -0.040 | +0.153 | -0.194 |  |
| P | GCG | A | +0.059 | +0.259 | -0.200 |  |
| P | GGC | G | -0.161 | +0.045 | -0.205 |  |
| P | GCA | A | -0.204 | +0.064 | -0.268 |  |
| P | GAT | D | -0.035 | +0.243 | -0.278 |  |
| P | TTT | F | +0.087 | +0.425 | -0.338 |  |
| P | CGA | R | -0.230 | +0.213 | -0.443 |  |
| E | TCC | S | +0.212 | -0.296 | +0.508 |  |
| E | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +1.195 | +0.701 | +0.494 | low-count (BWM) |
| E | GCC | A | -0.072 | -0.509 | +0.438 |  |
| E | TAC | Y | +0.108 | -0.272 | +0.380 |  |
| E | GTC | V | +0.062 | -0.286 | +0.348 |  |
| E | CAC | H | +0.126 | -0.208 | +0.334 |  |
| E | CGC | R | +0.061 | -0.192 | +0.254 |  |
| E | TCT | S | +0.202 | -0.030 | +0.232 |  |
| E | CTC | L | +0.042 | -0.182 | +0.224 |  |
| E | TTC | F | +0.055 | -0.166 | +0.220 |  |
| E | TGC | C | +0.075 | -0.081 | +0.156 |  |
| E | GCT | A | -0.046 | -0.201 | +0.155 |  |
| E | TCA | S | +0.218 | +0.089 | +0.128 |  |
| E | CAA | Q | +0.074 | -0.031 | +0.105 |  |
| E | AAC | N | +0.025 | -0.079 | +0.104 |  |
| E | CCA | P | -0.396 | -0.500 | +0.104 |  |
| E | GAC | D | -0.024 | -0.127 | +0.103 |  |
| E | ACA | T | +0.179 | +0.085 | +0.095 |  |
| E | TAT | Y | +0.084 | +0.042 | +0.043 |  |
| E | CAT | H | +0.135 | +0.099 | +0.036 |  |
| E | GTT | V | -0.047 | -0.035 | -0.011 |  |
| E | GGA | G | -0.213 | -0.195 | -0.018 |  |
| E | GAG | E | +0.050 | +0.080 | -0.030 |  |
| E | TCG | S | +0.290 | +0.323 | -0.033 |  |
| E | TTA | L | +0.143 | +0.179 | -0.037 |  |
| E | CTA | L | +0.129 | +0.175 | -0.047 |  |
| E | TTT | F | +0.075 | +0.134 | -0.058 |  |
| E | GAA | E | +0.058 | +0.122 | -0.064 |  |
| E | CGT | R | -0.089 | -0.022 | -0.067 |  |
| E | TTG | L | +0.123 | +0.202 | -0.079 |  |
| E | AGC | S | +0.065 | +0.153 | -0.088 |  |
| E | GAT | D | +0.066 | +0.155 | -0.089 |  |
| E | ATA | I | +0.165 | +0.316 | -0.151 |  |
| E | ATG | M | +0.083 | +0.235 | -0.152 |  |
| E | GTA | V | -0.077 | +0.086 | -0.163 |  |
| E | AGA | R | -0.125 | +0.043 | -0.169 |  |
| E | CAG | Q | +0.042 | +0.220 | -0.178 |  |
| E | GGC | G | -0.091 | +0.087 | -0.178 |  |
| E | AAG | K | -0.121 | +0.078 | -0.199 |  |
| E | ACG | T | +0.196 | +0.399 | -0.203 |  |
| E | TGG | W | -0.058 | +0.151 | -0.209 |  |
| E | TGT | C | +0.053 | +0.267 | -0.215 |  |
| E | AAT | N | +0.090 | +0.310 | -0.220 |  |
| E | CCG | P | -0.080 | +0.161 | -0.240 |  |
| E | CTG | L | +0.063 | +0.346 | -0.283 |  |
| E | CGA | R | +0.095 | +0.380 | -0.285 |  |
| E | GTG | V | -0.096 | +0.219 | -0.315 |  |
| E | GGT | G | -0.218 | +0.131 | -0.349 |  |
| E | AGT | S | +0.040 | +0.397 | -0.356 |  |
| E | AGG | R | -0.137 | +0.394 | -0.531 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only (n = 10 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | TTC | F | +0.079 | -0.008 | +0.087 |  |
| A | GTA | V | -0.107 | -0.014 | -0.093 |  |
| A | GCG | A | -0.214 | -0.023 | -0.190 |  |
| P | GAG | E | +0.073 | -0.011 | +0.084 |  |
| P | GTC | V | -0.032 | -0.008 | -0.025 |  |
| P | GCT | A | -0.054 | +0.011 | -0.064 |  |
| P | GGG | G | -0.201 | -0.023 | -0.178 |  |
| P | GGT | G | -0.340 | -0.006 | -0.333 |  |
| E | GCA | A | -0.057 | +0.020 | -0.077 |  |
| E | CCT | P | -0.233 | -0.019 | -0.213 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only (n = 31 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | GCC | A | -0.008 | -0.489 | +0.480 |  |
| A | CGC | R | -0.004 | -0.333 | +0.329 |  |
| A | CTC | L | -0.021 | -0.328 | +0.307 |  |
| A | CCG | P | -0.025 | -0.089 | +0.064 |  |
| A | CAG | Q | +0.012 | -0.045 | +0.057 |  |
| A | TCT | S | -0.000 | +0.037 | -0.038 |  |
| A | AGC | S | +0.031 | +0.115 | -0.084 |  |
| A | TTA | L | -0.014 | +0.195 | -0.209 |  |
| A | ACA | T | +0.004 | +0.222 | -0.219 |  |
| A | ACG | T | -0.009 | +0.235 | -0.245 |  |
| A | GAA | E | -0.012 | +0.265 | -0.277 |  |
| A | AAA | K | +0.005 | +0.338 | -0.333 |  |
| P | TGG | W | +0.013 | -0.207 | +0.219 |  |
| P | CAA | Q | +0.000 | -0.192 | +0.192 |  |
| P | CCC | P | +0.061 | -0.098 | +0.159 |  |
| P | TGC | C | +0.005 | -0.094 | +0.099 |  |
| P | AAC | N | -0.017 | -0.065 | +0.049 |  |
| P | TTC | F | +0.009 | +0.086 | -0.076 |  |
| P | GTG | V | +0.013 | +0.115 | -0.102 |  |
| P | CTA | L | +0.017 | +0.226 | -0.209 |  |
| P | GTT | V | -0.008 | +0.216 | -0.223 |  |
| E | ACC | T | +0.019 | -0.460 | +0.479 |  |
| E | ATC | I | +0.004 | -0.221 | +0.225 |  |
| E | ACT | T | -0.015 | -0.128 | +0.113 |  |
| E | CCC | P | -0.041 | -0.140 | +0.098 |  |
| E | CTT | L | +0.015 | -0.038 | +0.054 |  |
| E | ATT | I | -0.015 | +0.067 | -0.082 |  |
| E | AAA | K | -0.011 | +0.124 | -0.135 |  |
| E | GCG | A | +0.021 | +0.277 | -0.256 |  |
| E | GGG | G | -0.049 | +0.234 | -0.283 |  |
| E | CGG | R | +0.047 | +0.542 | -0.495 |  |

### Flag glossary

- `low-count` — at least one condition arm's day-5 / day-0 footprint count for this codon is below the stability threshold (BWM < 50 / control < 50); the parenthetical names which arm(s) tripped it (`BWM` = the BWM arm). In this file the flag fires only on the in-frame stop codon TGA, and only at the P and E sites where the BWM arm dips below threshold (`low-count (BWM)`); A:TGA clears the threshold and is unflagged. `log2_OR` (and therefore `effect change`) is unstable at small counts, so the large TGA values reflect sampling noise rather than biology.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 372 (186 per condition; 3 sites x ~62 codons each)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 329 (BWM 154/186, control 175/186)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 335
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow; 20 cells at exactly 0.0). Smallest non-zero p_adj is 2.762370e-296 (A, BWM, TAC).
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the millions has no meaningful floor; the dominant concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide).
- Per (condition, site) sig at FDR<0.05: BWM A 50/62, P 52/62, E 52/62; control A 59/62, P 56/62, E 60/62.
- Section split: 144 cells sig in both, 10 BWM-only, 31 control-only.

## Biological interpretation

**Near-universal FDR significance is a large-N artifact; the readable axis is `effect change`, not p (concrete).** 329 of 372 cells clear FDR<0.05 — essentially tied with the day_10_vs_day_0 codon file's 330 for the family's highest codon count — with pooled per-condition totals of roughly 2.0M-3.4M footprints per arm. At that N Fisher's exact returns a vanishing p for a trivially small composition shift, so which section a cell lands in (significant in both, BWM-only, or control-only) is a weak sorter and 20 cells underflow to exactly 0.0 simply because the p computation runs out of floating-point range. What this file is built to read is how differently the two conditions moved from day_0 to day_5 at each (site, codon) cell, captured by `effect change` = BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`: near zero is a shared trajectory, large magnitude is a divergence.

**The TGA stop spike is low-count instability, not a readthrough finding (concrete).** The in-frame stop codon TGA is enriched at day_5 in both arms at all three sites (A +0.503 / +0.466, P +1.111 / +0.909, E +1.195 / +0.701), and at the P and E sites the BWM arm dips below the stability threshold, so those two cells carry the `low-count (BWM)` flag. TGA is an in-frame stop, its odds ratio is dominated by the smallest cell, and its magnitude is the largest in the file at P and E — exactly the signature of small-count instability rather than biology. Quote its direction at most, never its magnitude, and never let it anchor the read.

**This contrast is shared-direction-dominant: most of the largest cells move the same way in both arms (concrete).** Unlike the two day-10 contrasts, where the biggest cells were genuine BWM-vs-control direction splits, the day_5-vs-day_0 step is dominated by co-directional cells. Among the large-magnitude cells with near-zero `effect change` are A:GGC and A:GGA both depleted, P:GGA depleted in both, and A:GAT, A:AAT, A:GTT all enriched in both; the glycine codons in particular move down together across sites. Because both arms carry these, they read as a condition-independent early-timecourse drift between day_0 and day_5 rather than a perturbation-specific effect — which is exactly what the within-condition binomial baseline can adjudicate.

**The genuine BWM-vs-control divergences are concentrated on arginine codons and are smaller than the day-10 splits (concrete).** The largest divergences are the arginine codon AGG at sites A and E (A:AGG BWM -0.202 / control +0.364, E:AGG BWM -0.137 / control +0.394): control enriches AGG from day_0 to day_5 while BWM depletes or stays flat. The serine codon TCC at E (BWM +0.212 / control -0.296) and the threonine codon ACC at A (BWM +0.144 / control -0.287) split the same way. These are the design-target reading, but their magnitudes are the muted version of the day-10 signal — `effect change` tops out near -0.57 (A:AGG) here. Whether the arginine split is carried by AGG specifically or shared across the arginine codon family is the cross-resolution question for the amino-acid parent.

**Whether the three contrasts decompose additively is the open cross-check, not a conclusion (exploratory).** This file is the day_0-anchored first leg of the timecourse; it cannot say on its own whether a day_5-vs-day_0 divergence persists, reverses, or accumulates by day_10. Reading it alongside `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5` and `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0` would show whether the A/E arginine split and the shared glycine depletion are additive (does day_10_vs_day_0 ~= day_5_vs_day_0 + day_10_vs_day_5 per cell?) or biphasic. The synonym-versus-amino-acid question — whether the AGG divergence survives aggregation in `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0` or is a single-synonym codon-usage shift — and the stable-baseline check against `codon_within_condition_binomial` are the other two directional hypotheses to carry forward, not findings this file establishes.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the shared-direction depletions (the glycine cluster A:GGC/A:GGA/P:GGA, A:CCA) and shared-direction enrichments (A:GAT, A:AAT, A:GTT) as a condition-independent day_0-to-day_5 drift; the BWM-vs-control arginine split at A:AGG and E:AGG (control enriches, BWM depletes) as the file's design-target divergence. *Exploratory*: whether the A/E arginine split is AGG-specific or amino-acid-level (cross-check the aa parent), and whether the day_5-vs-day_0 trajectory is additive or biphasic across the d10_vs_d5 and d10_vs_d0 sisters; the binomial baseline adjudicates the shared-direction cells. *Noise*: the in-frame stop TGA spike (low-count at P and E in BWM), the raw p magnitudes, and the near-universal significance count, all large-N or small-count artifacts rather than per-codon biology.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2x2 of (codon_count, total - codon_count) at day_5 vs day_0 within each condition. `global_codon_occ_stats.py` (Analysis 3b) pools the replicate counts per (condition, timepoint) before the test and applies BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of ~62 codons (corrected by `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`); each of the 6 (condition, site) sub-families is corrected independently, not across the full 372-row file. Effect is reported as `log2_OR` (log2 of the CSV `odds_ratio`): >0 = enriched at day_5 relative to day_0 within that condition, <0 = depleted. The test does not compare BWM against control directly (that is the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` reading carried by the paired columns) and does not test enrichment vs the transcriptomic background (the `within_condition_binomial` family). About 62 of 64 codons appear per (condition, site); the in-frame stop TGA is present, TAA/TAG are not retained in the significant set.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The 2x2 contingencies pool the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) before the test, so per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic and p-values run anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` / `effect change` as the effect, not a tiny p as extra certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled per-condition totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (day_5 ~2.01M BWM / ~2.41M control; day_0 ~2.11M BWM / ~3.37M control), so Fisher returns vanishing p for trivially small deviations — rank by effect, never by p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is applied within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of ~62 codons, not across the 372-test file, so two cells with the same raw p in different families can land at different `p_adj` — here significance is carried by section membership, not by a displayed p.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file cleanly answers "does day_5 differ from day_0 within this condition", leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to the side-by-side paired columns (the `effect change`), not to the test itself.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** The design-target reading: sense codons whose two conditions split direction over the timecourse (A:AGG, E:AGG, E:TCC, A:ACC, E:CGG, A:GCC) — read these from the paired BWM/control columns, not from either arm alone.
- **near-universal-sig-large-N.** 329/372 cells clear FDR<0.05, so which section a cell lands in (both / BWM-only / control-only) is weakly discriminating — rank by `|effect change|` within and across sections, not by section membership or p.
- **rare-codon-low-count.** The `low-count` flag (BWM < 50 / control < 50) fires only on the in-frame stop TGA, and only at P:TGA and E:TGA where the BWM arm is low (A:TGA clears the threshold and is unflagged); every sense codon clears the threshold, so for the flagged TGA rows quote direction, not the extreme magnitude.
- **stop-codon-instability.** TGA carries the file's largest `|log2_OR|` swings (E:TGA +1.195, P:TGA +1.111, A:TGA +0.503) on small counts in at least one arm — treat as low-support instability, not a sense-codon signal; it marks the same cells as `rare-codon-low-count`.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM A-site codon volcano (x = log2(odds ratio), about -1.2 to +1; y = -log10(FDR); glyphs coloured by encoded-residue class, triangles = FDR<0.05). The acidic codon GAT (red) at log2(OR) ~+0.35 and the polar AAT and valine GTT (purple / green) are the rightmost enrichments pinned near the top; a cluster of depletions sits at the top-left (the tryptophan codon TGG and the glycine codon GGC near -0.5, the cysteine codon TGC, the proline codon CCA), with the proline codon CCC (orange) a standout left-side depletion at ~-0.35. The arginine codon AGG (blue) sits lower-left (~-0.2). The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the lone far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+0.5 just above the FDR line; most sense codons cluster within +/-0.3 of zero.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM P-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The enrichments sit to the right — the leucine codon TTA and isoleucine ATA (green) near +0.25 to +0.33, with the serine codon TCG, threonine ACG (purple) and leucine CTG also right of zero — while the largest depletions are the glycine codons GGA and GGT and the arginine codon CGA to the left (~-0.2 to -0.4). Most sense codons are pinned near the top of the panel close to log2(OR)=0. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the lone far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+1.1 on a low -log10(FDR).](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM E-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The serine codons TCG / TCA / TCT / TCC (purple) and the threonine codons ACA / ACG cluster as the rightmost enrichments (~+0.2 to +0.29), while the proline codon CCA (orange) and the glycine codons GGA / GGT are the largest depletions to the left (~-0.2 to -0.4); the lysine codon AAG (blue) and arginine AGG sit just left of zero. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the lone far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+1.2 just above the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control A-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The proline codon CCA and the alanine codon GCC (orange / green) are the deepest depletions at the far top-left (~-0.49), with a band of smaller depletions below them (the glycine codon GGC, valine GTG, proline CCC); the enrichments to the right are led by the isoleucine codon ATA and the arginine codon AGG (~+0.36 to +0.43), with the arginine codon CGG and lysine AAA further right. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) sits near zero at the FDR threshold. Most cells are FDR<0.05 triangles.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control P-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The phenylalanine codon TTT (green) is the rightmost enrichment (~+0.43), with the isoleucine codon ATA, serine TCG and threonine ACG also right of zero; the largest depletions are the proline codon CCA, lysine AAG and threonine ACC to the left (~-0.34 to -0.40). The acidic codon GAA (red) sits high near zero. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+0.9 just above the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control E-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The arginine codon CGG (blue) is the deepest enrichment at the far right (~+0.54), with the arginine codon AGG and serine AGT (~+0.39) also right of zero; the largest depletions are the alanine codon GCC, proline CCA and threonine ACC at the far left (~-0.46 to -0.51). The acidic codon GAG (red) sits high near zero. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+0.7 just above the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
